project.name: BabyMon
project.description: Smart Evolving Parenting Companion - Mobile app with NestJS backend and Flutter frontend tracking baby development from conception through early childhood.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context filter that excludes node_modules, dist, .git, IDE files, environment files, test artifacts, and documentation from container builds to keep images lean.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the NestJS backend using Node.js 20, producing a minimal production image with Prisma client generation and a non-root user running on port 3000.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build with separate builder and production stages; production stage runs as non-root user (nestjs, uid 1001) and copies only compiled dist/ from builder.

nodes.name: .env
nodes.summary: Development environment configuration with database URL, JWT secrets, port, and trial period settings for local development.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Complete environment variable template documenting all required configuration including database, JWT, Stripe, AWS, rate limiting, and logging settings.

nodes.name: History.txt
nodes.summary: Change history log documenting the evolution of the BabyMon API backend.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project documentation with features overview, tech stack, quick start guide, API endpoints reference, testing instructions, subscription tiers, security compliance, and support information.

nodes.name: jest.config.js
nodes.summary: Jest testing configuration for the NestJS API application.

nodes.name: nest-cli.json
nodes.summary: NestJS CLI configuration specifying the module collection and source root directory.

nodes.name: package-lock.json
nodes.summary: Automatically generated lockfile pinning exact dependency versions for reproducible builds.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NestJS backend package manifest defining dependencies (NestJS, Prisma, Passport, Stripe, SendGrid, AWS SDK), dev tooling, and npm scripts for building, testing, seeding, and managing the Prisma database.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration enabling experimental decorators, emit metadata, commonjs modules targeting ES2021, with path aliases and source map output.

nodes.name: migration_lock.toml
nodes.summary: Prisma migration lock file ensuring consistent database provider across environments.

nodes.name: schema.prisma
nodes.summary: Prisma data model defining the core domain with 18 models: User, BabyMon, Milestone, FeedLog, HealthRecord, Badge, StageContent, Subscription, AuditLog, EntryChangeProposal, JournalProposal, StripeEvent, Media, GrowthRecord, LinkedAccount, LinkedBabyMon, RefreshToken, and PasswordResetToken with full relationship mappings.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: Core user identity entity with email, password hash, role, verification state, and cascade relationships to all major domain entities.

nodes.name: BabyMon
nodes.summary: Central child profile entity with gamification fields (currentXp, currentStage), conception tracking dates, physical attributes, and one-to-many relationships to milestones, feed logs, health records, badges, and media.

nodes.name: Milestone
nodes.summary: Parenting milestone entry with title, notes, media references, XP rewards, and soft delete support.

nodes.name: FeedLog
nodes.summary: Feeding event log with type, amount, unit, media references, and XP tracking for baby nutrition monitoring.

nodes.name: HealthRecord
nodes.summary: Health monitoring record with category, title, notes, media references, and XP rewards for baby health tracking.

nodes.name: Badge
nodes.summary: Gamification badge entity with badge type, name, icon, description, XP value, and unlock timestamp.

nodes.name: StageContent
nodes.summary: Pregnancy and post-birth stage content with summary, nurturing, and encouragement text organized by week/month numbers and XP thresholds.

nodes.name: Subscription
nodes.summary: User subscription entity with Stripe integration fields, trial period tracking, and tier management (CORE/AI).

nodes.name: AuditLog
nodes.summary: Audit trail logging event type, actor, payload JSON, IP address, and user agent for security compliance.

nodes.name: EntryChangeProposal
nodes.summary: Co-parent entry change proposal system with pending/approved/rejected workflow, proposed and original payloads, and auto-accept timing.

nodes.name: JournalProposal
nodes.summary: Journal entry change proposal linking to milestone/feed/health records with proposed changes and resolution tracking.

nodes.name: StripeEvent
nodes.summary: Stripe webhook event storage with eventId deduplication and processing status tracking.

nodes.name: Media
nodes.summary: Media asset entity storing S3 key, URL, thumbnail URL, file metadata, and relationships to both baby and user.

nodes.name: GrowthRecord
nodes.summary: Baby growth tracking with type (height/weight/head circumference), value, unit, measurement date, and notes.

nodes.name: LinkedAccount
nodes.summary: Co-parent linking between two users with pending/approved/rejected status and expiration tracking.

nodes.name: LinkedBabyMon
nodes.summary: User-to-BabyMon access relationship with EDIT/VIEW permission levels for shared baby profiles.

nodes.name: RefreshToken
nodes.summary: JWT refresh token storage with expiration, revocation tracking, and cascade deletion on user removal.

nodes.name: PasswordResetToken
nodes.summary: Password reset token with expiry and cascade deletion, enabling secure password recovery flows.

nodes.name: Device
nodes.summary: Push notification device registration tracking platform, device token, and last active timestamp.

nodes.name: migration.sql
nodes.summary: Initial database migration creating all core tables (User, BabyMon, Milestone, FeedLog, HealthRecord, Badge, StageContent, Subscription, AuditLog, EntryChangeProposal, StripeEvent, RefreshToken) with foreign keys and indexes.

nodes.name: migration.sql
nodes.summary: Database migration making stage content fields optional to support flexible content management.

nodes.name: app.module.ts
nodes.summary: Root NestJS module wiring together 22 feature modules, Prisma database, Pino logging, rate limiting with three-tier throttling, global throttler guard, and audit service.

nodes.name: main.ts
nodes.summary: Application bootstrap entry point configuring CORS, raw body middleware for Stripe webhooks, global prefix, validation pipe, Swagger documentation, and pino logging with startup banner.

nodes.name: seed.ts
nodes.summary: Database seeding script creating system user, default BabyMon profile, and pregnancy/post-birth stage content for all 40 pregnancy weeks and 24 post-birth months with personalized nurturing and encouragement text.

nodes.name: AdminModule
nodes.summary: NestJS module providing administrative endpoints for system management and monitoring.

nodes.name: AdminService
nodes.summary: Administrative service managing system-level operations including user management, audit log retrieval, and system health monitoring.

nodes.name: getAdminStats
nodes.summary: Retrieves aggregate system statistics for the admin dashboard.

nodes.name: getAuditLogs
nodes.summary: Fetches audit logs with filtering and pagination support.

nodes.name: getSystemHealth
nodes.summary: Checks system health and reports service status.

nodes.name: getAllUsers
nodes.summary: Lists all users with optional role filtering.

nodes.name: disableUser
nodes.summary: Disables a user account for administrative intervention.

nodes.name: enableUser
nodes.summary: Re-enables a previously disabled user account.

nodes.name: deleteUser
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes a user and their associated data.

nodes.name: admin.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Admin API controller exposing endpoints for system statistics, audit logs, user management, and health checks.

nodes.name: AppModule
nodes.summary: Root NestJS module composing all 22 feature modules with Prisma, logging, rate limiting, and audit infrastructure.

nodes.name: auth.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Authentication API controller providing registration, login, token refresh, email verification, password reset, logout, and profile endpoints.

nodes.name: AuthModule
nodes.summary: Authentication module wiring AuthService, JwtStrategy, Passport, and JWT service for token-based auth.

nodes.name: AuthService
nodes.summary: Core authentication service handling user registration with trial subscription, login with bcrypt verification, JWT token generation and refresh, email verification, password reset via email, logout with token revocation, and profile retrieval.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Registers a new user with password hashing, verification token, trial subscription creation, and verification email dispatch.

nodes.name: login
nodes.summary: Authenticates user credentials and returns access/refresh JWT tokens.

nodes.name: refreshTokens
nodes.summary: Validates and exchanges a refresh token for new access and refresh token pairs with token rotation.

nodes.name: verifyEmail
nodes.summary: Verifies a user's email using a time-limited verification token.

nodes.name: forgotPassword
nodes.summary: Initiates password reset flow by generating and emailing a reset token.

nodes.name: resetPassword
nodes.summary: Completes password reset by validating the token, hashing the new password, and revoking all existing sessions.

nodes.name: generateTokens
nodes.summary: Generates JWT access and refresh tokens and persists the refresh token to the database.

nodes.name: logout
nodes.summary: Revokes all or a specific refresh token for the current user session.

nodes.name: getProfile
nodes.summary: Retrieves the authenticated user's profile data.

nodes.name: validateUser
nodes.summary: Validates a user exists and is not deleted for use in JWT strategy.

nodes.name: JwtStrategy
nodes.summary: Passport JWT strategy extracting and validating access tokens for authenticated route protection.

nodes.name: RegisterDto
nodes.summary: Registration DTO with validated email and password fields.

nodes.name: LoginDto
nodes.summary: Login DTO with validated email and password input fields.

nodes.name: RefreshTokenDto
nodes.summary: Token refresh DTO with validated refreshToken string field.

nodes.name: BabyMonModule
nodes.summary: BabyMon feature module providing baby profile management endpoints.

nodes.name: BabyMonService
nodes.summary: Core baby profile service managing creation, stage progression, XP calculation, badge updates, and co-parent linking for BabyMon entities.

nodes.name: registerBabyMon
nodes.summary: Creates a new BabyMon profile with default stage content assignment.

nodes.name: getBabyMon
nodes.summary: Retrieves a specific BabyMon profile with related data.

nodes.name: updateBabyMon
nodes.summary: Updates BabyMon profile fields with ownership validation.

nodes.name: getMyBabyMons
nodes.summary: Lists all BabyMon profiles accessible to the current user including shared ones.

nodes.name: progressStage
nodes.summary: Advances the baby's stage with XP-based progression and badge evaluation.

nodes.name: awardXp
nodes.summary: Grants XP to a BabyMon and evaluates milestone stage transitions.

nodes.name: updateBadge
nodes.summary: Updates or creates a badge for the BabyMon with type deduplication.

nodes.name: BadgesModule
nodes.summary: Badges feature module for badge management endpoints.

nodes.name: BadgesService
nodes.summary: Badge management service for listing, granting, and removing badges on BabyMon profiles.

nodes.name: listBadges
nodes.summary: Lists all badges for a specific BabyMon profile.

nodes.name: grantBadge
nodes.summary: Grants a new badge to a BabyMon profile or updates existing one.

nodes.name: removeBadge
nodes.summary: Removes a badge from a BabyMon profile.

nodes.name: unlockBadgesByXp
nodes.summary: Automatically unlocks badges based on accumulated XP thresholds.

nodes.name: access-control.service.ts
nodes.summary: Access control service enforcing role-based permissions across the BabyMon domain.

nodes.name: auth.service.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for AuthService covering registration, login, token management, and password reset flows.

nodes.name: baby-mon.service.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for BabyMonService covering baby profile operations and stage progression.

nodes.name: public.decorator.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS decorator marking authentication endpoints as public, bypassing JWT guard enforcement.

nodes.summary: Initial User table with id, email, passwordHash, name, and audit timestamp columns.

nodes.name: AuditLog

nodes.summary: Badges API controller exposing badge listing, granting, and removal endpoints for BabyMon gamification.

nodes.name: admin.service.ts
nodes.summary: Administrative service for system management with 188 lines of logic for user administration, audit log retrieval, and health monitoring.

nodes.name: auth.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core authentication service with 346 lines handling the complete authentication lifecycle from registration to logout.

nodes.name: baby-mon.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core BabyMon profile service with 226 lines managing baby profiles, XP, stage progression, and badges.

nodes.name: baby-mon.controller.ts
nodes.summary: BabyMon API controller with endpoints for profile CRUD, stage progression, and XP management.

nodes.name: CreateBabyMonDto
nodes.summary: DTO for creating a BabyMon profile with validated name, gender, stage type, and optional date fields.

nodes.name: UpdateBabyMonDto
nodes.summary: DTO for updating BabyMon profile fields with partial update support.

nodes.name: StageContentDto
nodes.summary: DTO for stage content with summary, nurturing, and encouragement text fields.

nodes.summary: DTO definitions for BabyMon CRUD operations including profile creation, updates, and stage content.

nodes.name: badges.service.ts
nodes.summary: Badges service managing badge lifecycle with XP-based auto-unlock for BabyMon gamification.

nodes.name: access-control.types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for the access control system including permission enums and guard strategy types.

nodes.name: UserRole
nodes.summary: User role enum (USER, ADMIN, SYSTEM) for role-based access control.

nodes.name: Permission
nodes.summary: Permission enum defining granular access levels across BabyMon resources.

nodes.name: audit-event.enum.ts
nodes.summary: Audit event type enumeration covering user lifecycle, BabyMon CRUD, milestone/feed/health record operations, subscription changes, and access management.

nodes.name: AuditEvent
nodes.summary: Enum of all auditable events: USER_REGISTERED, USER_LOGIN, USER_LOGOUT, BABYMON_CREATED, BABYMON_UPDATED, BABYMON_DELETED, MILESTONE_CREATED, MILESTONE_UPDATED, FEED_LOG_CREATED, FEED_LOG_UPDATED, FEED_LOG_DELETED, HEALTH_RECORD_CREATED, HEALTH_RECORD_UPDATED, HEALTH_RECORD_DELETED, SUBSCRIPTION_CREATED, SUBSCRIPTION_CANCELLED, ACCESS_GRANTED, ACCESS_REVOKED.

nodes.name: audit.service.ts
nodes.summary: Audit logging service for recording system events with structured payload storage and query support.

nodes.name: AuditService
nodes.summary: Service for creating and querying audit log entries with event type categorization and metadata.

nodes.name: log
nodes.summary: Creates an audit log entry with event type, actor, resource metadata, and IP/user-agent tracking.

nodes.name: getLogs
nodes.summary: Queries audit logs with optional event type and user filters.

nodes.name: current-user.decorator.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS decorator extracting the current authenticated user from the JWT payload.

nodes.name: public.decorator.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS decorator marking endpoints as public to bypass authentication guards.

nodes.name: roles.decorator.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS decorator for role-based access control, specifying required roles on endpoint methods.

nodes.name: pagination.dto.ts
nodes.summary: DTO with validated page and limit parameters for paginated API responses.

nodes.name: PaginationDto
nodes.summary: Pagination input DTO with optional page and limit fields, defaults to page=1, limit=50.

nodes.name: jwt-auth.guard.ts
nodes.summary: JWT authentication guard verifying bearer tokens and attaching user to the request context.

nodes.name: roles.guard.ts
nodes.summary: Role-based authorization guard checking user roles against route-level decorator metadata.

nodes.name: subscription-write.guard.ts
nodes.summary: Guard enforcing subscription-based write access, blocking unauthenticated write operations.

nodes.name: audit.interceptor.ts
nodes.summary: NestJS interceptor intercepting all HTTP requests to automatically audit logging with event determination, babyMon ID extraction, and resource type classification.

nodes.name: AuditInterceptor
nodes.summary: Interceptor implementing cross-cutting audit logging by mapping HTTP methods and paths to audit events, extracting babyMon IDs from params/body/query, and delegating to AuditService.

nodes.name: intercept
nodes.summary: Core interception logic timing requests, determining audit events, and recording to both console and database.

nodes.name: determineEvent
nodes.summary: Maps HTTP method and path to corresponding AuditEvent enum values using pattern matching.

nodes.name: extractBabyMonId
nodes.summary: Extracts babyMonId from route params, query params, or request body.

nodes.name: extractResourceId
nodes.summary: Extracts resource ID from route params or response body for audit tracking.

nodes.name: determineResourceType
nodes.summary: Determines the resource type (BabyMon, Milestone, FeedLog, etc.) from the request path.

nodes.summary: Evolution API controller for tracking baby developmental milestones and progress tracking.

nodes.summary: Exports BabyMon data as CSV with section headers and proper escaping for complex fields.

nodes.name: exportBabyMon
nodes.summary: Generates an HTML bento-box style report with badges, traits, and recent activity cards.

layers.name: API Backend
layers.description: NestJS backend application with Docker build configuration, environment settings, and testing infrastructure

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Prisma schema definitions, database migrations, development database, and seeding logic for the shared PostgreSQL database

layers.name: Mobile Client
layers.description: Flutter mobile app with feature-based architecture (auth, dashboard, feeding, health, journal, milestones, profile, tracking), each organized into data/domain/presentation layers with Riverpod state management, Drift local database, and Dio API client

layers.name: iOS Test Project
layers.description: Swift/iOS test application with MVVM architecture containing authentication, home, messaging, photo upload, badge display, and stats viewing screens

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project specification, technical architecture, and operational documentation including AWS deployment, design specs, testing plans, implementation plans, and team communication artifacts

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Container orchestration via Docker Compose for PostgreSQL and pgAdmin, multi-stage Dockerfile for the API, and GitHub Actions CI pipeline for automated testing and building of both backend and Flutter applications

layers.name: Project Support
layers.description: Mobile app development documentation, Git configuration, Claude Code settings, and AWS deployment reference material that support the development workflow

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Welcome to BabyMon, a smart parenting companion that tracks baby development from conception through early childhood (up to 24 months). This project is a monorepo with a NestJS backend API and a Flutter mobile app. Read the README to understand the feature set -- baby profiles, stage tracking, milestones, feeding logs, health records, and an evolution system with XP and badges. The app follows a core/AI subscription model with a 14-day trial.
tour.languageLesson: Monorepo structure with apps/api and apps/mobile directories means you should cd into each app to run its respective dev server, tests, and builds independently.

tour.title: Project Specification
tour.description: SPEC.md defines the project requirements and scope. This document connects to the infrastructure layer via docker-compose.yml, showing how the development environment supports both the API and mobile app. Reviewing the spec alongside the README gives you the full picture of what BabyMon is supposed to do and how it is architected.
tour.languageLesson: Technical documentation in monorepos should sit in a docs/ directory at the root level, separate from app-specific README files. This keeps cross-cutting concerns visible without cluttering individual app directories.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Local Dev
tour.description: The infrastructure trio -- Docker Compose, Dockerfile, and the CI pipeline -- defines how BabyMon runs in development and production. docker-compose.yml orchestrates the PostgreSQL database. The NestJS Dockerfile uses multi-stage builds to compile TypeScript in a builder stage and ship a minimal Alpine image. The GitHub Actions CI pipeline gates on docker-compose, ensuring database-dependent tests pass before deployment.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose is used to spin up PostgreSQL for local development, while a separate Dockerfile defines production containerization. CI pipelines that depend on docker-compose can validate database migrations and integration tests against the same data layer used in local dev.

tour.title: Database Schema & Migrations
tour.description: The data layer is the foundation of the entire application. The Prisma schema (schema.prisma) defines the type-safe data model that both the NestJS backend and Flutter mobile app rely on. The migration.sql file applies the initial schema to PostgreSQL. The seed.ts script populates the database with initial data, enabling you to run the app immediately after setup. All backend models, DTOs, and queries flow from this schema through Prisma's type generation.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma generates TypeScript types from your schema.prisma file. When you modify the schema, run 'prisma generate' to regenerate types, then 'prisma migrate dev' to create and apply a migration. The seed script runs after migrations via 'prisma db seed'.

tour.title: Backend Configuration & Manifest
tour.description: The NestJS backend has its own package.json for dependency management, tsconfig.json for TypeScript compilation, and nest-cli.json for the NestJS build toolchain. The .env.example file documents required environment variables (database URL, JWT secrets, Stripe keys), while .env holds the actual values. Understanding these config files is essential before you can start the backend server locally.
tour.languageLesson: NestJS uses nest-cli.json to configure module resolution and build output paths. The .env.example file should list all required environment variables as comments so developers know what to set up before running the backend.

tour.title: API Backend Architecture
tour.description: The API Backend layer (per the project's layer definition) contains NestJS controllers, services, modules, guards, and decorators. This is the server-side logic that the Flutter mobile app communicates with via REST API. While the graph's code import edges are sparse, the layer description tells us this is where business logic lives: authentication with JWT, baby profile management, milestone tracking, feeding logs, health records, and subscription handling via Stripe.

tour.title: Mobile App Entry Point
tour.description: The Flutter app starts with main.dart, which bootstraps the application. It loads the app.dart root widget and initializes core services including the service locator (GetIt for dependency injection) and the GoRouter for navigation. Understanding this entry point gives you the map of how the app's layers connect -- from DI setup through API client initialization to route configuration.
tour.languageLesson: Flutter apps use Provider, Riverpod, or GetIt for dependency injection. BabyMon uses GetIt as its service locator, which is a simple registration/resolution pattern ideal for apps that need to access services from anywhere in the widget tree.

tour.title: Core Infrastructure & API Client
tour.description: Before diving into features, the app's core infrastructure handles cross-cutting concerns. The service locator (service_locator.dart) registers all app services with GetIt. The Dio HTTP client (api_client.dart) configures the base URL, interceptors for JWT tokens, and error handling. The API service layer (api_service.dart) wraps HTTP calls into typed methods. These three files form the backbone that every feature screen depends on.

tour.title: Navigation & Routing
tour.description: The navigation layer sits between the app entry point and the feature screens. app_router.dart configures GoRouter with route definitions for all app screens. main_screen.dart provides the main navigation shell (likely a BottomNavigationBar) that holds the authenticated user's experience. Together, these files define the app's information architecture and how users move between the tracking, feeding, health, milestones, journal, and profile sections.

tour.title: Authentication Feature
tour.description: Auth is the gateway to the app. The user entity defines the data model for users. The auth_provider manages login state with Riverpod. The login and register screens implement the onboarding flow. Auth state is shared across all features via the service locator, and JWT tokens are handled by the API client interceptors from Step 8.

tour.title: Core Feature Screens
tour.description: BabyMon's core parenting workflows live in the remaining feature screens. Dashboard shows the home overview, tracking handles pregnancy/post-birth week-by-week monitoring, feeding logs record breastmilk/formula/solid feedings, and milestones captures developmental achievements with photos. The health, journal, and profile screens complete the feature set by managing vaccination records, the unified journey feed, and user settings respectively.
tour.languageLesson: Feature-based architecture groups code by capability (auth, feeding, health) rather than by technical concern. Each feature directory contains its own domain entities, state management, and presentation screens, making it easy to find and modify feature-specific code without touching unrelated parts of the app.

tour.title: iOS Test Project & Mobile Docs
tour.description: The project includes a separate iOS test project with Swift files (TestProjectApp.swift as the SwiftUI entry point, AuthViewModel.swift for auth logic) that is independent from the Flutter app. These likely serve as a proof-of-concept or parallel development track. The design spec and AWS deployment guide provide additional context for the mobile development process and cloud infrastructure strategy.
